texto: But only a few have the will -- be it born of courage or recklessness, folly or sublime wisdom -- to act and, by their action, transform themselves. They will pay for their courage, of course; the world does not treat its "others" lightly. But so will the rest of us -- the ones who love them -- haunted as we are by our envy of their bright, burning beauty, which we can bear neither to look at nor to turn away from.
explicacao: Citação de Ed Tarkington sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “But only a few have the will -- be it born of courage or recklessness, folly or sublime wisdom --…” articula coragem, transformation, courage em registro de coragem, com relevância para contextos como mudanca e transformation.
